moment_text: As you shared, I spent 20 years in fashion and loved my career until I didn't. And really what became that turning point for me was, you know, when you are come from humble beginnings and you work super hard to get to finally that corner office, and it was, I think of my 23, 20 4-year-old self. Like the dream was, I'm gonna get to that job. I'm gonna go sit in that seat and I finally was there. And when you're in that moment and you're seeing the success that you're having, and you've got this team of a hundred people underneath you and you know, you're continuing to, to, to really shine. And then there's also the aspect too of, there's a huge paycheck that goes around it. There's the ego of, I've got this title, but at the end of the day. There were multiple things that were falling apart outside of the job itself because I put career as the number one, and you always hear the phrase, you gotta put your oxygen, oxygen mask on first. And I completely, I failed at that. What was happening outside of the day to day was I was completely burnt out. I was leaving my house every day, 5 30, 6 o'clock in the morning. I was getting home if I was lucky, eight 30 or nine o'clock at night. And I was barely seeing my kids during the week. I love being a mom, but I also love being a working mom, so I know that that is one of the things I will continue to always be that working mom. It makes me a better mom, and I was missing out on too much in my kids' lives. There were first days of school, there were end of school, there were concerts, there were sports, there was everything that I was missing out on, and in my marriage, I was missing out on key parts of my marriage with my husband, and then the pieces I wasn't taking care of myself. For three years. In that last job, I never went to the dentist. I never went to the gynecologist. I never had a mammogram. I missed out on all of those things. And so the oxygen mask wasn't happening and I wasn't feeling fulfilled.

I can't let this, like, I can't let all these things have me step out of what. Was what I believed was a sign of success and I was having a break. I truly, there was a breakdown.
